name: US Yield Curve 10Y-2Y Spread
notes: Inversion (negative) has preceded every US recession 6-18m in advance

name: US Yield Curve 10Y-3M Spread
notes: Campbell & Shiller preferred recession predictor; stronger near-term signal

name: US M2 Money Supply
notes: YoY growth leads nominal GDP by 12-18m; negative YoY = deflation risk

name: US Building Permits (SAAR)
notes: Leads housing starts 1-2m; YoY negative = construction contraction

name: US Initial Jobless Claims 4-Week Average
notes: Sustained rise above 300k signals labour market deterioration

name: US Nonfarm Payrolls MoM Change
notes: 3m avg >200k = solid expansion; <100k = slowdown; negative = recession

name: US Unemployment Rate
notes: Sahm Rule: 0.5pp rise in 3m average vs prior 12m low = recession signal

name: US Industrial Production Index
notes: YoY negative = recession signal; companion to manufacturing PMI

name: US Retail Sales ex-Autos MoM
notes: Consumer spending momentum; YoY real negative = recessionary signal

name: US SLOOS Net % Banks Tightening C&I Lending Standards (Large Firms)
notes: Net tightening >20% has preceded every recession; 6-12m lead time

name: Chicago Fed National Financial Conditions Index (NFCI)
notes: Negative = easier than avg; positive and rising = tightening; leads growth 3-6m

name: US CPI Headline
notes: Primary public inflation benchmark; >5% YoY = high-inflation regime

name: US Core CPI (ex Food & Energy)
notes: Strips transitory commodity effects; sticky >3% = overheating signal

name: US Core PCE Deflator
notes: Fed's preferred inflation measure; Fed 2% target refers to this series

name: US PPI All Commodities
notes: Leads CPI by 2-3m; acceleration = leading indicator of consumer price pressure

name: US TIPS 5Y Breakeven Inflation Rate
notes: Market-implied 5yr avg inflation; level >3% = inflation concern

name: US TIPS 10Y Breakeven Inflation Rate
notes: Most widely watched breakeven; rising 5Y vs 10Y = near-term inflation concerns

name: US 5Y5Y Forward Inflation Swap Rate
notes: Market-implied inflation years 5-10; measures whether long-run expectations anchored

name: UMich Consumer Inflation Expectations 1Y
notes: Consumer-based; Fed watches closely; rise >3.5% would be policy alarm

name: US Federal Funds Rate (Effective)
notes: Foundational Tier 1 indicator; direction and pace determine regime trajectory

name: US 2-Year Treasury Yield (Constant Maturity)
notes: Short-end policy-sensitive nominal rate; used for 2s10s slope and to track where markets price the Fed terminal rate

name: US 10-Year Treasury Yield (Constant Maturity)
notes: Benchmark long-end nominal rate; input to yield-curve slope, breakeven, and term-premium indicators; drives mortgage pricing

name: US 10Y Real Interest Rate (TIPS)
notes: Real rates >2% historically associated with growth decel and recession risk

name: US 5Y Real Interest Rate (TIPS)
notes: Short-end real rate; rising = tightening financial conditions for risk assets

name: US HY Credit Spread OAS (ICE BofA)
notes: >600bps = severe stress; >800bps = crisis; tightening from stress = risk-on

name: US IG Credit Spread OAS (ICE BofA)
notes: >200bps historically coincides with recessions; IG-HY ratio = risk appetite

name: ICE BofA US Corporate Index Effective Yield
notes: All-in effective yield on broad US IG corporate bond index; level reflects risk appetite and funding costs for IG issuers

name: ICE BofA US Corporate Index Total Return Index Value
notes: Total return index for US IG corporates; captures coupon income plus price return; use for momentum and drawdown signals

name: SLOOS: Net Tightening — C&I Loans Small Firms
notes: Companion to DRTSCILM (large firms). Divergence = dual-speed credit cycle.

name: SLOOS: Net Tightening — Credit Card Loans
notes: Consumer credit availability. Tightening leads consumer spending slowdown.

name: SLOOS: Net Tightening — Consumer Loans (ex Credit Card & Auto)
notes: Auto/student/personal loan standards. Broadens consumer credit picture.

name: SLOOS: Net Tightening — CRE Loans (Nonfarm Nonresidential)
notes: CRE credit cycle; leads commercial property stress by 2-4 quarters.

name: UMich Consumer Sentiment (Overall)
notes: Headline consumer confidence; below 70 historically correlated with recessions.

name: Conference Board Consumer Confidence (US)
notes: Business-sourced survey; leading indicator for consumer spending and employment.

name: US Business Confidence
notes: OECD business confidence for US; companion to consumer confidence.

name: Philadelphia Fed: Mfg Business Conditions (General Activity)
notes: One of the earliest monthly US mfg surveys; leads ISM by ~1 week.

name: Empire State (NY Fed): Mfg Business Conditions
notes: First regional Fed mfg survey released each month. Closely watched ISM preview.

name: Richmond Fed: Mfg Business Conditions (Composite Index)
notes: Mid-Atlantic manufacturing survey. Covers VA, MD, DC, NC, SC, WV.

name: Kansas City Fed: Mfg Business Conditions (Composite Index)
notes: Tenth Federal Reserve District. Covers OK, KS, NE, CO, WY, NM, MO.

name: Dallas Fed: Mfg Business Conditions (General Activity)
notes: Eleventh Federal Reserve District. Covers TX, northern LA, southern NM.

name: World Bank Iron Ore Price Index (USD)
notes: World Bank iron ore price; proxy for China's steel and infrastructure demand; leads industrial metals and EM commodity exporters

name: Consumer Confidence
notes: OECD/EC consumer confidence balance statistic. Negative = below long-run average; leading indicator for household spending.

name: UK 10-Year Gilt Yield
notes: UK benchmark long-rate; tracks BoE policy expectations and UK fiscal risk premium; used for UK-Bund spread analysis

name: Germany 10-Year Bund Yield
notes: Core euro area reference rate and risk-free anchor for EUR bonds; used for intra-European sovereign spread analysis

name: Germany Business Confidence
notes: OECD business tendency survey composite for Germany; above 100 = above-trend optimism

name: UK Business Confidence
notes: OECD business tendency survey composite for UK; quarterly frequency

name: Japan Business Confidence
notes: OECD business tendency survey composite for Japan; above 100 = above-trend optimism

name: France Business Confidence
notes: OECD business tendency survey composite for France; above 100 = above-trend optimism

name: Italy Business Confidence
notes: OECD business tendency survey composite for Italy; above 100 = above-trend optimism

name: China Business Confidence
notes: OECD business tendency survey composite for China; above 100 = above-trend optimism

name: Germany Consumer Confidence
notes: OECD consumer opinion survey composite for Germany; below 100 = below-trend pessimism

name: UK Consumer Confidence
notes: OECD consumer opinion survey composite for UK; below 100 = below-trend pessimism

name: Japan Consumer Confidence
notes: OECD consumer opinion survey composite for Japan; below 100 = below-trend pessimism

name: China Consumer Confidence
notes: OECD consumer opinion survey composite for China; below 100 = below-trend pessimism
